name: Source Fidelity review for Senomorphics bundle
summary: The staged source, intervention, study, finding, registry refreshes, and outlook wording stay within the checked primary-source boundaries. PMID 42251040 supports the mianserin/Ca2+/SASP claim as human aging/progeria-derived cell evidence plus progeroid and naturally aged mouse healthspan/lifespan evidence, not treated-human efficacy. ClinicalTrials.gov confirms NCT03103893 remains completed with actual 2017-11-30 completion, 36 actual enrollment, rapamycin intervention, and no registry results section; NCT03107884 remains active, not recruiting, with estimated 2026-11-01 completion, 64 actual enrollment, metformin/placebo arms, and no registry results section. The staged trial notes correctly separate peer-reviewed result sources from registry-result status.

findings.claim_or_issue: PMID 42251040 supports the staged claim that mianserin attenuated senescence in cells derived from HGPS patients and naturally aging humans, improved aging phenotypes, and significantly extended lifespan in LmnaG609G/G609G progeroid and naturally aged mice.
findings.why_it_matters: This is a material senomorphics source, but its direct efficacy evidence is animal and cell-system evidence rather than a treated-human aging trial.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; retain the animal evidence tier, human-derived cell boundary, low confidence, and no-treated-human caveat.

findings.claim_or_issue: The source identifies mianserin as a tetracyclic antidepressant and describes HTR2B/2C antagonism lowering Ca2+ concentrations; the staged record does not claim an approved aging indication and flags aging-use-preclinical status.
findings.why_it_matters: The intervention can be tracked as a repurposing candidate only if public wording keeps the aging claim separate from non-aging drug use.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; keep the aging-use-preclinical, human-aging-dose-and-safety-unresolved, and senomorphic-attribution-unresolved risk flags.

findings.claim_or_issue: ClinicalTrials.gov lists NCT03103893 as completed, last updated 2021-09-22, with actual start 2017-09-25, actual primary and study completion 2017-11-30, 36 actual participants, phase 1/2, rapamycin intervention, and no registry results section.
findings.why_it_matters: The local study can keep posted peer-reviewed results through PMID 31761958, but the registry itself should not be described as having posted ClinicalTrials.gov results.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; the staged horizon note already says the peer-reviewed result is in the evidence layer while ClinicalTrials.gov has no results section.

findings.claim_or_issue: ClinicalTrials.gov lists NCT03107884 as active, not recruiting, last updated 2025-12-05, with actual start 2019-08-01, estimated primary and study completion 2026-11-01, 64 actual participants, early phase 1 status, metformin/placebo arms, and no registry results section.
findings.why_it_matters: The published older-adult bed-rest substudy remains valid public evidence, but the parent registry remains open and should not be treated as a fully completed registry result.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; keep the staged parent-registry horizon note and recheck after the estimated 2026-11-01 completion date.
